rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.title: Maasai Shuka (source)
results.notes: Source-domain page only; not an LLM watermark artefact.

results.title: Stylometric Watermarks for LLMs (2024/05)
results.notes: DIRECT FUNCTIONAL TWIN: stylometric features (acrostica, sensorimotor, sentence-level signals) used as watermark carrier in LLM output. Same primitive as SHUKA-STYLE-MARK (stylometric watermark).

results.title: Ensemble Watermarks for LLMs (ACL 2025)
results.notes: Combines stylometric (acrostica + sensorimotor) features with red-green watermark for 97.75% detection. Directly overlaps stylometric carrier.

results.title: Ensemble Watermarks for LLMs (2024/11)
results.notes: Same ensemble stylometric + token-bias watermark, different venue. Stylometric watermark primitive present.

results.title: Stylometry recognizes human vs LLM short texts (2025/07)
results.notes: Stylometric distinguisher between human and LLM short samples — same artefact (stylometric signal in LLM output detectable post-hoc).

results.title: Watermarking LMs through token sampling (2025/06)
results.notes: Token-sampling watermark — adjacent watermark primitive (different carrier, same goal of LLM output identity signal).

results.title: Watermarking for LLMs: A Survey (2025)
results.notes: LLM watermarking survey — covers stylometric and related primitives; broad coverage hits semantic and functional thresholds.

results.title: LLM Fingerprinting via Semantically Conditioned Watermarks (2025/05)
results.notes: Semantically-conditioned watermark for LLM fingerprinting — adjacent watermark primitive for ownership/identity (same goal as candidate's identity-signal claim).

fail_reason: Candidate SHUKA-STYLE-MARK is not novel. Direct functional twins exist: 'Stylometric Watermarks for LLMs' (arxiv 2405.08400) and 'Ensemble Watermarks for LLMs' (ACL 2025.acl-long.145 / arxiv 2411.19563) already use stylometric features (sentence-level signals, sensorimotor norms, acrostica) as the watermark carrier in LLM outputs — the exact primitive (stylometric-feature-only watermark) the candidate claims as novel. 7 of 8 results are hits under the rule (kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7), driven by 7 semantic hits and 7 functional hits; with total_hits=7 >= 1, the candidate FAILS the novelty test. The claimed differentiator ('null-space, no-token-bias') is not sufficient to escape the prior-art twin since existing stylometric watermark work already covers stylometric-feature carriers.
